titleStmt.title: Letter from James Lawler to Robert Chalmers, 26 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Lawler

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from James Lawler, the clerk for the Navan Urban District Council to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858–1938). After the Easter rising of 1916 Chalmers was sent to Ireland as under-secretary, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), his chief secretary. In the letter Lawler writes on behalf of the Council to request permission to end the appointment of the Office of Shop Inspector, which he says costs the Council an unnecessary £10 a year.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/8988/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Navan Urban District Council
Navan , 26 May 1916 Sir I am directed by my Council
to ask if they may dispense with
the office of Shop Inspectors. The appointment was made in
the ordinary Course of a few years
ago and still continues but
it is considered unnecessary and
the Council would like to save
the £10 per year paid in connection
therewith. The Local Government Board
having been written on the Subject
referred the Council to His Excellency
The Lord Lieutenant. I am Sir
Your Ob Servant James Lawler
Clerk The Under Secretary
The Castle
Dublin Reply the council
to see 13(1) of
the act which
requires that
an inspector
shall be
appointed to
enforce the
permissions of
the act
EOF
27-5-16 Done LH 30/5
A 30/5
